description: Decline path triggered by single-borrower exposure limit breach, NOT credit quality. Ridgecrest Health is a strong BBB+ borrower; the $15M equipment loan pushes committed exposure to 8.6% of Tier 1 capital, exceeding the 8.0% limit. Rules engine fires limit_status=breach and drives decline regardless of strong financials.

pipeline_steps.expected: risk_band=1-pass (credit quality strong); exposure_limit_flag=true — independent of credit rating
Memo documents strong credit quality separately from exposure limit breach; DECLINE recommendation driven by regulatory limit, not credit quality; cites OCC 12 CFR 32

decline_reasons: Single-borrower exposure limit breach: $73.5M committed (8.65%) exceeds 8.0% Tier 1 capital limit
OCC 12 CFR Part 32 lending limit applies

critical_test_assertion: Decision must be DECLINE despite 1-pass credit rating — validates that exposure rules gate independently of credit quality rating
demo_note: SYNTHETIC SCENARIO — tests that single-borrower-exposure rule produces decline independent of credit quality; validates regulatory-rules gate in workflow
